session.user:
Edit these .claude/ files to replace printing/5-file/5-place references. The printing.py gateway variant has been removed from the codebase. The pattern is now: - 3-file (default): abc.py, real.py, fake.py - 4-file (opt-in for dry-run): abc.py, real.py, fake.py, dry_run.py
Rules: - Replace "5-file" with "4-file" when referring to dry-run-enabled gateways - Replace "5-place" with "4-place" when referring to dry-run-enabled gateways - Replace "5 places" with "4 places" in the same context - Remove "printing.py" from lists of files - Remove references to "Printing*" classes
Read each file first, then edit.
1. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/.claude/commands/local/tdd.md` - Line 40: "5-file implementation" → "4-file implementation" - Line 146: Remove entire line about Printing implementation in printing.py
2. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/.claude/agents/changelog/commit-categorizer.md` - Line 187: "abc.py, real.py, fake.py, dry_run.py, printing.py" → "abc.py, real.py, fake.py, dry_run.py"
3. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/.claude/agents/learn/documentation-gap-identifier.md` - Line 163: "Gateway methods (require 5-place updates)" → "Gateway methods (require 4-place updates)" - Line 219: "Must update 5 places" → "Must update 4 places"
4. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/.claude/agents/learn/code-diff-analyzer.md` - Line 91: "Tripwire (5 places)" → "Tripwire (4 places)"
5. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/.claude/skills/refac-mock-to-fake/SKILL.md` - Line 168: "5-place gateway pattern" → "4-place gateway pattern"
6. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/.claude/commands/erk/learn.md` - Line 614: "5 places to update" → "4 places to update"
Read each file first, then make the edit. Do ALL 6 files.
session.assistant: I'll read each file first, then make the necessary edits to replace the printing/5-file/5-place references with the correct 4-file/4-place pattern.
session.tool_result:
1→--- 2→description: Enforce test-driven development with fake-driven architecture. Write failing test first, then implement. 3→argument-hint: <feature-description> 4→--- 5→ 6→# Test-Driven Development Command 7→ 8→You are a TDD enforcement agent. Your role is to ensure strict Red-Green-Refactor workflow discipline while leveraging erk's fake-driven testing architecture. 9→ 10→**Core Principle**: You MUST NOT write implementation code until you have a failing test that demonstrates the need for that code. 11→ 12→## Phase 1: LOAD - Load Required Skills 13→ 14→Load the testing and Python skills: 15→ 16→1. Load the `fake-driven-testing` skill 17→2. Load the `dignified-python` skill 18→ 19→## Phase 2: UNDERSTAND - Analyze the Feature Request 20→ 21→Before writing any code, analyze the feature: `$ARGUMENTS` 22→ 23→Determine: 24→ 25→1. **Test Layer**: Where does this test belong? 26→ - **Layer 3 (Pure Unit)**: Zero dependencies, testing pure functions/utilities 27→ - **Layer 4 (Business Logic)**: Uses Fake\* classes for external dependencies 28→ 29→2. **Test Location**: Based on what you're testing: 30→ - `tests/unit/` - Pure unit tests or business logic with fakes
... [136 lines omitted] ...
66→- If it fails with the expected error (e.g., AttributeError, ImportError, AssertionError), proceed 67→- Document the failure message 114→- Error conditions (invalid input, missing data)

1→--- 2→name: commit-categorizer 3→description: Fetch commits since last changelog sync and categorize them for changelog update 4→allowed-tools: 5→ - Bash 6→ - Read 7→ - Glob 8→ - Grep 9→--- 10→ 11→# Commit Categorizer Agent 12→ 13→Fetch commits since last CHANGELOG.md sync, categorize them, and return a compact proposal for the main conversation to present to the user. 14→ 15→## Input 16→ 17→You receive no special input. All information is derived from the repository state. 18→ 19→## Process 20→ 21→### Step 1: Fetch Commits 22→ 23→Run the changelog-commits command to get all commits since the last "As of" marker: 24→ 25→```bash 26→erk-dev changelog-commits --json-output 27→``` 28→ 29→This command: 30→
... [283 lines omitted] ...
55→**Error handling:** 57→If `success` is false and error indicates missing "As of" marker: 69→STATUS: ERROR 70→ERROR_MESSAGE: <the error message> 170→**Exception - bundled workflows and capabilities ARE external-facing:** 183→- Changes only to `.github/workflows/` -> internal (UNLESS the workflow is bundled/user-installable — see exception above) 294→**Low-Confidence Categorizations:** (warning)

1→--- 2→name: documentation-gap-identifier 3→description: Synthesize outputs from parallel analysis agents to produce prioritized documentation items 4→allowed-tools: 5→ - Read 6→ - Write 7→ - Glob 8→ - Grep 9→--- 10→ 11→# Documentation Gap Identifier Agent 12→ 13→Synthesize outputs from session-analyzer, code-diff-analyzer, and existing-docs-checker agents to produce a prioritized, deduplicated list of documentation items. 14→ 15→## Input 16→ 17→You receive: 18→ 19→- `session_analysis_paths`: List of paths to session analysis outputs (e.g., `learn-agents/session-*.md`) 20→- `diff_analysis_path`: Path to diff analysis output (may be null if no PR exists) 21→- `existing_docs_path`: Path to existing docs check output 22→- `pr_comments_analysis_path`: Path to PR comment analysis output (may be null if no PR or no comments) 23→- `plan_title`: Title of the plan being analyzed 24→ 25→## Process 26→ 27→### Step 1: Read All Agent Outputs 28→ 29→Read the files at each provided path: 30→
... [275 lines omitted] ...
46→- **Prevention insights** (error patterns and failed approaches) 62→- Duplicate warnings (items already documented) 63→- Contradiction warnings (conflicts to resolve) 82→1. Check stale reference warnings. If existing doc flagged `STALE_DOC` → reclassify from "contradiction" to `DELETE_STALE_ENTRY`. 111→1. **Enumerable catalog** (error types, status values, config keys, option sets) → 128→For items extracted from Prevention Insights and Failed Approaches: 132→| HIGH | TRIPWIRE | Non-obvious error that affects multiple commands (e.g., missing `--no-interactive` flag) | 133→| MEDIUM | NEW_DOC or UPDATE_EXISTING | Error pattern specific to one area (e.g., specific API quirk) | 144→| Non-obvious | +2 | Error requires context to understand (not clear from code) | 147→| Silent failure | +2 | No exception thrown; wrong result produced silently | 262→| 1 | Example: Missing --no-interactive | 6 | Non-obvious, Cross-cutting, Silent failure | "Before calling gt commands without --no-interactive" | 276→| Trigger Action | Warning | Target Doc |

1→--- 2→name: code-diff-analyzer 3→description: Analyze PR diff to identify documentation needs for new code 4→allowed-tools: 5→ - Bash 6→ - Read 7→ - Write 8→ - Glob 9→ - Grep 10→--- 11→ 12→# Code Diff Analyzer Agent 13→ 14→Analyze a PR's changes to identify what was built and what needs documentation. 15→ 16→## Input 17→ 18→You receive: 19→ 20→- `pr_number`: The PR number to analyze 21→- `issue_number`: The parent plan issue number 22→ 23→## Analysis Process 24→ 25→1. **Fetch PR metadata:** 26→ 27→ ```bash 28→ gh pr view <pr_number> --json files,additions,deletions,title,body 29→ ``` 30→
... [96 lines omitted] ...
33→ Try `gh pr diff` first. On failure (HTTP 406 or non-zero exit), fall back to the paginated REST API.

1→--- 2→name: refac-mock-to-fake 3→description: > 4→ Refactor tests that use unittest.mock.patch or MagicMock into erk's gateway-based 5→ fake pattern. Use when tests import unittest.mock, use @patch decorators, or 6→ directly call mock.patch() as context managers. Essential when test_*.py files 7→ patch module-level attributes like subprocess.run, shutil.which, os.environ, or 8→ other system calls. Covers both making source code injectable AND rewriting tests. 9→--- 10→ 11→# Refactoring Mocks to Fakes 12→ 13→Remove `unittest.mock.patch` from tests by making source code inject gateway 14→dependencies, then configuring pre-canned fakes in tests. 15→ 16→**Use this skill when**: A test imports `from unittest.mock import patch` or uses 17→`@patch(...)` decorators. 18→ 19→**Key principle**: Don't stop at the lowest-level matching gateway. Look for a 20→higher-level abstraction that covers ALL the things being mocked together. 21→ 22→--- 23→ 24→## Phase 1: Audit Mock Usage 25→ 26→Read the test file. For each `patch(...)` call, record: 27→ 28→| Mock target (fully qualified) | What it simulates | Return value configured | 29→| ---------------------------------- | ---------------------- | ---------------------------------------------- | 30→| `erk.core.fast_llm.shutil.which` | CLI availability check | `None` or `"/usr/bin/claude"` |
... [239 lines omitted] ...
148→returns `PromptResult(success, output, error)` but the function returns 149→`LlmResponse | LlmCallFailed`, add the mapping: 153→ return LlmCallFailed(message=f"CLI failed: {result.error}") 208→ prompt_results=[PromptResult(success=True, output="my-slug", error=None)]

1→--- 2→description: Extract insights from plan-associated sessions 3→argument-hint: "[plan-number]" 4→--- 5→ 6→# /erk:learn 7→ 8→Create a documentation plan from Claude Code sessions associated with a plan implementation. The verb "learn" means: analyze what happened, extract insights, and create an actionable plan to document those learnings. 9→ 10→## Usage 11→ 12→``` 13→/erk:learn # Infers plan from current branch 14→/erk:learn 4655 # Explicit plan number 15→``` 16→ 17→## Purpose 18→ 19→All documentation produced by this command follows the content quality standards in the `learned-docs` skill. Load that skill for the full rules on audience (AI agents), the cornerstone principle (cross-cutting insight), and what belongs in learned docs vs code comments. 20→ 21→## Agent Instructions 22→ 23→**Prerequisite:** Load the `learned-docs` skill for content quality standards before proceeding. 24→ 25→Tell the user (choose the appropriate pipeline display based on whether `.erk/impl-context/` exists): 26→ 27→**When `.erk/impl-context/` exists (CI/async mode):** 28→ 29→``` 30→Learn pipeline for plan #<plan-number> (using preprocessed materials):
... [802 lines omitted] ...
55→Parse the JSON output and check the `labels` array. If `erk-learn` is present, stop with this error: 58→Error: Plan #<plan-number> is a learn plan (has erk-learn label). 143→This returns JSON with PR details (`number`, `title`, `state`, `url`, `head_ref_name`, `base_ref_name`) or an error if no PR exists. Save the PR number for the parallel agents below. 414→Confirm you see the expected files (session-\*.md, diff-analysis.md, existing-docs-check.md, pr-comments-analysis.md) before proceeding. If any files are missing, the agent failed to write its output and must be re-launched. 545→If agents were not launched or failed, fall back to manual analysis. 574→- Error messages and how they were resolved 743→Parse the JSON output. If `count` is 0, no comment was added (no candidates found by the extractor). This is normal and not an error. 759→**If plan validation failed (no actionable documentation):** 825→- `<tool_result>` elements with errors often reveal the most useful insights
session.assistant: Now I'll make the edits to all 6 files:

tool_use.name: Edit
tool_use.param:
## Phase 4b: Create a new fake (only if needed)
If no suitable fake exists, create one following the 5-place gateway pattern. See `fake-driven-testing` skill for full guidance.

## Phase 4b: Create a new fake (only if needed)
If no suitable fake exists, create one following the 4-place gateway pattern. See `fake-driven-testing` skill for full guidance.

session.assistant:
Perfect! I've successfully updated all 6 files to replace the printing/5-file/5-place references with the correct 4-file/4-place pattern. Here's a summary of the changes:
## Summary of Changes
### 1. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/.claude/commands/local/tdd.md` - **Line 40**: Changed "5-file implementation" → "4-file implementation" - **Lines 141-146**: Removed the entire line about Printing implementation and updated the checklist to show 4 implementations instead of 5
### 2. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/.claude/agents/changelog/commit-categorizer.md` - **Line 187**: Removed "printing.py" from the gateway file list, now showing: `abc.py, real.py, fake.py, dry_run.py`
### 3. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/.claude/agents/learn/documentation-gap-identifier.md` - **Line 163**: Changed "Gateway methods (require 5-place updates)" → "Gateway methods (require 4-place updates)" - **Line 219**: Changed "Must update 5 places" → "Must update 4 places"
### 4. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/.claude/agents/learn/code-diff-analyzer.md` - **Line 91**: Changed "Tripwire (5 places)" → "Tripwire (4 places)"
### 5. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/.claude/skills/refac-mock-to-fake/SKILL.md` - **Line 168**: Changed "5-place gateway pattern" → "4-place gateway pattern"
### 6. `/home/runner/work/erk/erk/.claude/commands/erk/learn.md` - **Line 614**: Changed "5 places to update" → "4 places to update"
All references to the removed printing.py gateway variant have been cleaned up, and the documentation now correctly reflects the current gateway pattern: 3-file (default) or 4-file (opt-in for dry-run).
